html:
The unfolding US-Iran war deal dominates global attention today, marked by fundamental disagreements over nuclear inspections and a politically charged standoff between Congress and Trump, even as US sanctions relief has enabled a swift rebound in Iranian oil exports. This complex détente is reshaping regional alliances and global energy markets, while facing skepticism from Iran and criticism from Israel.

US-Iran Deal: UN nuclear chief Rafael Grossi seeks inspections, but an Iranian minister insists access requires a final deal. The US temporarily lifted oil sanctions, boosting Iran's exports. Congress passed a war powers measure against Trump, leading to a confrontation with Senator Bill Cassidy. Secretary of State Marco Rubio defends the deal on a Gulf tour; US and Iranian technical teams meet June 29-30. Iran views the deal as a "declaration of US defeat."

Russia-Ukraine: Ukrainian long-range strikes caused a major power outage in Crimea's Sevastopol and hit a Russian natural gas plant. Belarus reportedly shut down drone signal repeaters ahead of a Zelenskyy ultimatum. European leaders pledged unity before a NATO summit, following British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's resignation. Trump also told NATO chief Mark Rutte that NATO nations "failed" the US in the Iran war.

Middle East Security: A UN report alleges Israeli killings of Gaza children post-truce amount to genocide, which Israel denies. In Lebanon, Benjamin Netanyahu vowed no troop withdrawal "as long as I am PM" after Israeli army strikes. A civilian contractor with an IDF unit died from a Gaza building collapse. The war crimes trial for Wassim al-Assad, a Syrian president's cousin, has begun.

Global Economy: Iran's oil exports rebounded due to lifted US sanctions; UAE exports hit 85% of pre-war levels, supported by an Oman-UN corridor in the Strait of Hormuz. Australia projects reduced transmission line needs from booming home battery adoption. Europe faces holiday travel delays from its new border system. Russia's Parliament approved tax amendments for fuel shortages.

Political Developments: New York's Democratic primary saw a sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates, reflecting Israel-Gaza war divisions and solidifying anti-Zionist politics. In Colombia, right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella defeated left-wing Senator Iván Cepeda, promising stronger ties with Israel. Federal judges also ruled against Trump administration policies, including the SAVE voter tool.

Health & Environment: A severe Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, with a record caseload, led to France's first confirmed case and Kenya halting a US-backed quarantine center. Extreme heat is causing record European temperatures, resulting in French power outages and health warnings. In tech, the US opened a second investigation into a fatal Tesla crash, and intelligence partners warn AI could breach defenses soon.
